title: Discipline Peer Tutoring
description: The ARC Learning Assistants (LAs) will be offering peer tutoring support for students enrolled in NYU Shanghai courses! The tutoring is available on a drop-in basis only. text:
Discipline Peer Tutoring
The ARC Learning Assistants (LAs) will be offering peer tutoring support for students enrolled in NYU Shanghai courses! The tutoring is available on a drop-in basis only. Simply check the schedule and visit the ARC during the listed times for your course. Our front desk Information Assistant will gladly connect you with a Learning Assistant for a 20-minute session. Please note that tutoring is offered exclusively in person at the ARC W523.
Who We Are
ARC Learning Assistants (LA) are NYU Shanghai student tutors who provide discipline-specific academic support for NYU Shanghai students out of the classroom. LAs work closely with faculty and ARC professionals to support student success and promote independent and active learning. LAs provide Individual and small-group tutoring in a variety of STEM, Business, Economics, and Chinese Language courses.
What We Do？
Facilitate your insights into the course content and your learning process
Support you in identifying and solving learning problems on your own
Help you review key concepts and strengthen your problem-solving skills
Provide relevant learning information, recommend useful learning resources, and share personal learning experiences
Encourage your motivation and help you build academic confidence
What We Don’t Do？
Complete or correct coursework for you, or provide direct answers
Assist you with any materials identified as tests, whether take-home, in-class, or otherwise
Go over the entire lectures that you may have missed
Predict grades for assignments or exams
Advise on course selections or whether to continue in a particular course
Spring 2026 LA-supported Courses
Mathematics: Precalculus, Calculus, Linear Algebra, Honors Linear Algebra II, Multivariable Calculus, Foundations of Mathematical Methods, Probability and Statistics, Ordinary Differential Equations, Honors Analysis I
Computer Science & Engineering: Intro to Computer Programming, Introduction to Computer and Data Science, Data Structures, Databases, Operating Systems, Machine Learning, Discrete Mathematics, Computer Architecture, Algorithms
Natural Science: General Physics II, Foundations of Biology I, Foundations of Chemistry II, Organic Chemistry II
Neural Science: Cellular and Molecular Neuroscience, Introduction to Linguistics: The Science of Human Language
Business: Statistics for Business and Economics, Foundations of Finance, Principles of Financial Accounting, Corporate Finance, Introduction to Marketing
Economics: Principles of Macroeconomics, Microeconomics, Intermediate Macroeconomics, Economics of Global Business, Econometrics
Chinese Language: Elementary & Intermediate & Advanced Chinese
No. LA tutoring is available on a drop-in basis only. Please refer to the tutoring schedule and stop by during the designated times for the course you need help with.
If there are no other students waiting, you may extend your session by an additional 20 minutes. However, we do not recommend staying with the same LA for more than three consecutive sessions. It’s important to take time to process and apply what you've learned before returning for further help.
There’s no limit on the number of sessions you can attend for different subjects. However, for the same subject, you may attend up to three consecutive sessions or a maximum of six sessions total per day.
Yes, you’re welcome to drop in with friends if you’re working on the same questions or topics. If no other students are waiting, the session time may be slightly extended. However, we don’t recommend large groups, as it can make it harder for everyone to get the support they need.
If no LA is available when you arrive, please wait in the designated waiting area or try coming back during another shift. If your course is not currently supported by our LA program, you can let the information assistant at the front desk know. We regularly assess student demand and may adjust the list of LA-supported courses in the future based on feedback.
Yes, review sessions may be offered before exams, but not necessarily for all LA-supported courses. The decision depends on feedback from LAs, student attendance, and requests from students or course instructors.
